adversarial_check.description: Parse-and-gate simulation beyond the 9 listed commands: programmatically extracted the json examples from both standard/AGF.md and tools/SKILL.md, confirmed both use the canonical sibling-array shape (final_verdict contains ONLY 'approved'; issues/missing_acceptance_coverage/unsupported_claims are top-level siblings), then ran the documented predicate (approved===true AND all three arrays empty) against each. AGF.md's example (approved=true) correctly passes. SKILL.md's example intentionally documents approved=false as a generic shape template (not asserted to pass) and correctly fails as-is; flipping only its approved value to true and re-running the identical predicate then correctly passes, proving the documented shape and predicate genuinely compose into a working parser rather than only looking consistent in prose. Finally, mutated a copy of the now-passing doc with one non-empty issues[] entry (approved left true) and confirmed the gate correctly rejects it, demonstrating the arrays are load-bearing, not decorative.
